---
title: "Fitting a Q-Learning Model to Matching Pennies Data"
---

We fit a reinforcement-learning model from
[Lee et al.](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.tics.2004.09.003) that maintains
two **action values**, $V_L$ and $V_R$ — one for each side the agent can
choose.  These values track how good each action has been recently, and are
used to drive the agent's choice on every trial.

### Interactive demo

Use the widget below to step through a **two-armed bandit** task.
On each trial the agent chooses Left or Right.  One side has an 80% chance
of reward while the other gives 0% — and the rewarded side **switches
every 20 trials** (shown by background shading in the history plot).
Watch how the action values $V_L$ and $V_R$ evolve, how the sigmoid
converts them into a choice probability, and how the agent adapts after
a block switch.  Adjust the sliders to see how different parameter
settings change behaviour.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

viewof alpha = Inputs.range([0.01, 0.99], {value: 0.5, step: 0.01, label: "α (decay)"})
viewof delta1 = Inputs.range([0, 3], {value: 1.0, step: 0.1, label: "δ₁ (reward)"})
viewof delta2 = Inputs.range([-3, 0], {value: -1.0, step: 0.1, label: "δ₂ (no reward)"})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

mutable state = ({
  vL: 0, vR: 0, history: [],
  _stepN: 0, _step10N: 0, _backN: 0, _resetN: 0
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

viewof backBtn = Inputs.button("Back")
viewof stepBtn = Inputs.button("Next Trial")
viewof step10Btn = Inputs.button("+10 Trials")
viewof resetBtn = Inputs.button("Reset")
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// Style the buttons
html`<style>
  .ql-demo button {
    padding: 6px 18px;
    font-size: 13px;
    font-weight: 600;
    color: white;
    border: none;
    border-radius: 6px;
    cursor: pointer;
  }
  .ql-demo form:nth-child(1) button { background: #888; }
  .ql-demo form:nth-child(2) button { background: #2A9DF4; }
  .ql-demo form:nth-child(3) button { background: #6366F1; }
  .ql-demo form:nth-child(4) button { background: #888; }
</style>
<div class="ql-demo" style="display:flex; gap:8px; margin-bottom:8px;">
  ${viewof backBtn}
  ${viewof stepBtn}
  ${viewof step10Btn}
  ${viewof resetBtn}
</div>`
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// Two-armed bandit: the high-reward side (80%) switches every ~20 trials
function banditReward(choice, trialNum) {
  const blockLen = 20;
  const highSide = Math.floor(trialNum / blockLen) % 2 === 0 ? "R" : "L";
  if (choice === highSide) {
    return Math.random() < 0.8;
  }
  return false;
}

function runStep(s) {
  const pR = 1 / (1 + Math.exp(-(s.vR - s.vL)));
  const chooseRight = Math.random() < pR;
  const choice = chooseRight ? "R" : "L";
  const trialNum = s.history.length;
  const rewarded = banditReward(choice, trialNum);
  const blockLen = 20;
  const highSide = Math.floor(trialNum / blockLen) % 2 === 0 ? "R" : "L";
  const d = rewarded ? delta1 : delta2;
  const newVL = chooseRight ? alpha * s.vL : alpha * s.vL + d;
  const newVR = chooseRight ? alpha * s.vR + d : alpha * s.vR;
  const entry = ({
    trial: trialNum + 1,
    vL_before: s.vL, vR_before: s.vR, pR,
    choice, rewarded, highSide,
    vL_after: newVL, vR_after: newVR
  });
  return ({vL: newVL, vR: newVR, entry});
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false

// All handlers in one cell so Quarto doesn't render extra outputs
{
  // Back one trial
  const s0 = state;
  if (backBtn > s0._backN && s0.history.length > 0) {
    const last = s0.history[s0.history.length - 1];
    mutable state = ({
      vL: last.vL_before, vR: last.vR_before,
      history: s0.history.slice(0, -1),
      _stepN: s0._stepN, _step10N: s0._step10N,
      _backN: backBtn, _resetN: s0._resetN
    });
  } else if (backBtn > s0._backN) {
    mutable state = ({...s0, _backN: backBtn});
  }

  // Single step
  const s1 = state;
  if (stepBtn > s1._stepN) {
    const r = runStep(s1);
    mutable state = ({
      vL: r.vL, vR: r.vR,
      history: [...s1.history, r.entry],
      _stepN: stepBtn, _step10N: s1._step10N,
      _backN: s1._backN, _resetN: s1._resetN
    });
  }

  // +10 steps
  const s2 = state;
  if (step10Btn > s2._step10N) {
    let cur = ({vL: s2.vL, vR: s2.vR, history: [...s2.history],
                _stepN: s2._stepN, _step10N: s2._step10N,
                _backN: s2._backN, _resetN: s2._resetN});
    for (let i = 0; i < 10; i++) {
      const r = runStep(cur);
      cur = ({...cur, vL: r.vL, vR: r.vR, history: [...cur.history, r.entry]});
    }
    mutable state = ({...cur, _stepN: s2._stepN, _step10N: step10Btn,
                      _backN: s2._backN, _resetN: s2._resetN});
  }

  // Reset
  const s3 = state;
  if (resetBtn > s3._resetN) {
    mutable state = ({
      vL: 0, vR: 0, history: [],
      _stepN: stepBtn, _step10N: step10Btn,
      _backN: backBtn, _resetN: resetBtn
    });
  }
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  const w = Math.min(width, 700);
  const h = 340;
  const margin = ({top: 30, right: 30, bottom: 30, left: 50});
  const pw = w - margin.left - margin.right;
  const ph = h - margin.top - margin.bottom;

  const currentVL = state.vL;
  const currentVR = state.vR;
  const currentPR = 1 / (1 + Math.exp(-(currentVR - currentVL)));

  const svg = d3.create("svg")
    .attr("viewBox", `0 0 ${w} ${h}`)
    .attr("width", w).attr("height", h)
    .style("font-family", "Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif")
    .style("max-width", "100%");

  // ── Left panel: value bars ──
  const barW = 50;
  const barAreaW = pw * 0.28;
  const barX = margin.left + barAreaW / 2;
  const barMaxH = ph * 0.7;
  const barBaseline = margin.top + ph * 0.5;

  const maxVal = Math.max(Math.abs(currentVL), Math.abs(currentVR), 1.5);
  const barScale = (barMaxH / 2) / maxVal;

  svg.append("text")
    .attr("x", barX).attr("y", margin.top + 2)
    .attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("font-size", 12)
    .attr("font-weight", 600).attr("fill", "#333")
    .text("Action values");

  svg.append("line")
    .attr("x1", barX - barW * 1.5).attr("x2", barX + barW * 1.5)
    .attr("y1", barBaseline).attr("y2", barBaseline)
    .attr("stroke", "#999").attr("stroke-width", 0.5);

  const vlH = currentVL * barScale;
  svg.append("rect")
    .attr("x", barX - barW - 8).attr("width", barW)
    .attr("y", vlH >= 0 ? barBaseline - vlH : barBaseline)
    .attr("height", Math.max(Math.abs(vlH), 0.5))
    .attr("fill", "#2A9DF4").attr("rx", 3).attr("opacity", 0.85);
  svg.append("text")
    .attr("x", barX - barW / 2 - 8).attr("y", margin.top + 20)
    .attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("font-size", 12)
    .attr("fill", "#2A9DF4").attr("font-weight", 600)
    .text(`V_L = ${currentVL.toFixed(2)}`);

  const vrH = currentVR * barScale;
  svg.append("rect")
    .attr("x", barX + 8).attr("width", barW)
    .attr("y", vrH >= 0 ? barBaseline - vrH : barBaseline)
    .attr("height", Math.max(Math.abs(vrH), 0.5))
    .attr("fill", "#F45B69").attr("rx", 3).attr("opacity", 0.85);
  svg.append("text")
    .attr("x", barX + barW / 2 + 8).attr("y", margin.top + 20)
    .attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("font-size", 12)
    .attr("fill", "#F45B69").attr("font-weight", 600)
    .text(`V_R = ${currentVR.toFixed(2)}`);

  // ── Right panel: sigmoid curve ──
  const sigX0 = margin.left + barAreaW + 30;
  const sigW = pw - barAreaW - 30;
  const sigH = ph * 0.7;
  const sigY0 = margin.top + 16;

  svg.append("text")
    .attr("x", sigX0 + sigW / 2).attr("y", margin.top + 2)
    .attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("font-size", 12)
    .attr("font-weight", 600).attr("fill", "#333")
    .text("Choice probability (sigmoid)");

  const xRange = 5;
  const sx = d3.scaleLinear().domain([-xRange, xRange]).range([sigX0, sigX0 + sigW]);
  const sy = d3.scaleLinear().domain([0, 1]).range([sigY0 + sigH, sigY0]);

  svg.append("line")
    .attr("x1", sigX0).attr("x2", sigX0 + sigW)
    .attr("y1", sy(0.5)).attr("y2", sy(0.5))
    .attr("stroke", "#ddd").attr("stroke-dasharray", "3,3");

  const nPts = 100;
  const sigPoints = d3.range(nPts + 1).map(i => {
    const z = -xRange + (2 * xRange * i / nPts);
    return ({x: z, y: 1 / (1 + Math.exp(-z))});
  });
  const line = d3.line().x(d => sx(d.x)).y(d => sy(d.y)).curve(d3.curveBasis);
  svg.append("path")
    .datum(sigPoints).attr("d", line)
    .attr("fill", "none").attr("stroke", "#333").attr("stroke-width", 2);

  const currentZ = currentVR - currentVL;
  const clampedZ = Math.max(-xRange, Math.min(xRange, currentZ));
  svg.append("circle")
    .attr("cx", sx(clampedZ)).attr("cy", sy(currentPR))
    .attr("r", 7).attr("fill", "#F45B69").attr("stroke", "white")
    .attr("stroke-width", 2);

  svg.append("line")
    .attr("x1", sx(clampedZ)).attr("x2", sx(clampedZ))
    .attr("y1", sy(currentPR)).attr("y2", sy(0))
    .attr("stroke", "#F45B69").attr("stroke-dasharray", "3,3").attr("opacity", 0.5);
  svg.append("line")
    .attr("x1", sigX0).attr("x2", sx(clampedZ))
    .attr("y1", sy(currentPR)).attr("y2", sy(currentPR))
    .attr("stroke", "#F45B69").attr("stroke-dasharray", "3,3").attr("opacity", 0.5);

  svg.append("text")
    .attr("x", sx(clampedZ)).attr("y", sy(0) + 16)
    .attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("font-size", 10).attr("fill", "#666")
    .text(`V_R−V_L = ${currentZ.toFixed(2)}`);
  svg.append("text")
    .attr("x", sigX0 - 6).attr("y", sy(currentPR) + 4)
    .attr("text-anchor", "end").attr("font-size", 11).attr("fill", "#F45B69")
    .attr("font-weight", 600)
    .text(`P(R)=${currentPR.toFixed(2)}`);

  for (const v of [0, 0.5, 1]) {
    svg.append("text")
      .attr("x", sigX0 - 6).attr("y", sy(v) + 4)
      .attr("text-anchor", "end").attr("font-size", 9).attr("fill", "#999")
      .text(v.toFixed(1));
    svg.append("line")
      .attr("x1", sigX0 - 3).attr("x2", sigX0)
      .attr("y1", sy(v)).attr("y2", sy(v)).attr("stroke", "#999");
  }

  svg.append("line")
    .attr("x1", sigX0).attr("x2", sigX0)
    .attr("y1", sy(0)).attr("y2", sy(1))
    .attr("stroke", "#333").attr("stroke-width", 1);
  svg.append("line")
    .attr("x1", sigX0).attr("x2", sigX0 + sigW)
    .attr("y1", sy(0)).attr("y2", sy(0))
    .attr("stroke", "#333").attr("stroke-width", 1);

  return svg.node();
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  const recent = state.history.slice(-60);
  if (recent.length === 0) return html`<p style="color:#999; font-style:italic; font-size:13px;">
    Press <b>Next Trial</b> to begin.</p>`;

  const w = Math.min(width, 700);
  const h = 230;
  const margin = ({top: 10, right: 15, bottom: 65, left: 50});
  const pw = w - margin.left - margin.right;
  const ph = h - margin.top - margin.bottom;

  const svg = d3.create("svg")
    .attr("viewBox", `0 0 ${w} ${h}`)
    .attr("width", w).attr("height", h)
    .style("font-family", "Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif")
    .style("max-width", "100%");

  const trials = recent.map(d => d.trial);
  const sx = d3.scaleLinear()
    .domain([d3.min(trials) - 0.5, d3.max(trials) + 0.5])
    .range([margin.left, margin.left + pw]);
  const sy = d3.scaleLinear().domain([0, 1]).range([margin.top + ph, margin.top]);

  // Block shading: highlight which side is the high-reward side
  let blockStart = recent[0].trial;
  let blockSide = recent[0].highSide;
  for (let i = 1; i <= recent.length; i++) {
    const cur = i < recent.length ? recent[i] : null;
    if (cur === null || cur.highSide !== blockSide) {
      const x1 = sx(blockStart - 0.5);
      const x2 = sx((cur ? cur.trial : recent[recent.length - 1].trial + 1) - 0.5);
      svg.append("rect")
        .attr("x", x1).attr("y", sy(1))
        .attr("width", x2 - x1).attr("height", sy(0) - sy(1))
        .attr("fill", blockSide === "R" ? "#F45B69" : "#2A9DF4")
        .attr("opacity", 0.07);
      if (cur) { blockStart = cur.trial; blockSide = cur.highSide; }
    }
  }

  svg.append("line")
    .attr("x1", margin.left).attr("x2", margin.left + pw)
    .attr("y1", sy(0.5)).attr("y2", sy(0.5))
    .attr("stroke", "#ddd").attr("stroke-dasharray", "3,3");

  const line = d3.line().x(d => sx(d.trial)).y(d => sy(d.pR));
  svg.append("path")
    .datum(recent).attr("d", line)
    .attr("fill", "none").attr("stroke", "#333").attr("stroke-width", 1.5);

  for (const d of recent) {
    const color = d.choice === "R" ? "#F45B69" : "#2A9DF4";
    const yPos = d.choice === "R" ? sy(0.95) : sy(0.05);
    svg.append("circle")
      .attr("cx", sx(d.trial)).attr("cy", yPos).attr("r", 3.5)
      .attr("fill", d.rewarded ? color : "none")
      .attr("stroke", color).attr("stroke-width", 1.5);
  }

  svg.append("line")
    .attr("x1", margin.left).attr("x2", margin.left)
    .attr("y1", sy(0)).attr("y2", sy(1)).attr("stroke", "#333");
  for (const v of [0, 0.5, 1]) {
    svg.append("text")
      .attr("x", margin.left - 6).attr("y", sy(v) + 4)
      .attr("text-anchor", "end").attr("font-size", 9).attr("fill", "#999")
      .text(v.toFixed(1));
  }
  svg.append("text")
    .attr("x", margin.left - 6).attr("y", margin.top + ph / 2)
    .attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("font-size", 11).attr("fill", "#333")
    .attr("transform", `rotate(-90, ${margin.left - 35}, ${margin.top + ph / 2})`)
    .text("P(R)");

  svg.append("line")
    .attr("x1", margin.left).attr("x2", margin.left + pw)
    .attr("y1", sy(0)).attr("y2", sy(0)).attr("stroke", "#333");
  svg.append("text")
    .attr("x", margin.left + pw / 2).attr("y", h - 4)
    .attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("font-size", 11).attr("fill", "#333")
    .text("Trial");

  // Legend — below the plot
  const lgY = h - 30;
  const lg = svg.append("g")
    .attr("transform", `translate(${margin.left}, ${lgY})`);

  // Row 1: choices
  lg.append("circle").attr("cx", 0).attr("cy", 0).attr("r", 5)
    .attr("fill", "#2A9DF4").attr("stroke", "#2A9DF4");
  lg.append("text").attr("x", 10).attr("y", 5)
    .attr("font-size", 12).attr("fill", "#555").text("L choice");

  lg.append("circle").attr("cx", 100).attr("cy", 0).attr("r", 5)
    .attr("fill", "#F45B69").attr("stroke", "#F45B69");
  lg.append("text").attr("x", 110).attr("y", 5)
    .attr("font-size", 12).attr("fill", "#555").text("R choice");

  lg.append("circle").attr("cx", 210).attr("cy", 0).attr("r", 5)
    .attr("fill", "none").attr("stroke", "#888").attr("stroke-width", 1.5);
  lg.append("text").attr("x", 220).attr("y", 5)
    .attr("font-size", 12).attr("fill", "#555").text("= no reward");

  // Row 1 continued: block shading
  lg.append("rect").attr("x", 340).attr("y", -7).attr("width", 16).attr("height", 14)
    .attr("fill", "#F45B69").attr("opacity", 0.2).attr("rx", 2);
  lg.append("text").attr("x", 360).attr("y", 5)
    .attr("font-size", 12).attr("fill", "#555").text("R rewarded");

  lg.append("rect").attr("x", 460).attr("y", -7).attr("width", 16).attr("height", 14)
    .attr("fill", "#2A9DF4").attr("opacity", 0.2).attr("rx", 2);
  lg.append("text").attr("x", 480).attr("y", 5)
    .attr("font-size", 12).attr("fill", "#555").text("L rewarded");

  return svg.node();
}
```

### Value update

After each trial, the value of the **chosen** side $c$ (where $c$ is either
$L$ or $R$) is updated:

$$
V_c \leftarrow \alpha \, V_c + \delta
$$

- $\alpha \in (0,1)$ is a **decay / memory** parameter — it controls how
  quickly old outcomes are forgotten.  Values close to 1 mean long memory;
  values close to 0 mean only the most recent trial matters.
- $\delta$ depends on the outcome:
    - $\delta_1$ is added after a **reward** (typically positive, reinforcing
      the chosen action).
    - $\delta_2$ is added after **no reward** (typically negative,
      discouraging the chosen action).
- The **unchosen** side's value is not shifted by $\delta$ — it simply
  decays: $V_u \leftarrow \alpha \, V_u$.

### Choice rule

On each trial the agent picks Right with probability given by the
**sigmoid** (logistic) function applied to the value difference:

$$
P(R) = \frac{1}{1 + \exp(-(V_R - V_L))}
$$

When $V_R > V_L$ the agent is more likely to choose Right; when
$V_L > V_R$ it favours Left; when the values are equal the choice is
a coin flip ($P(R) = 0.5$).

### Parameters

The three free parameters $(\alpha, \delta_1, \delta_2)$ are fit by
maximising the log-likelihood (see the
[Fundamentals page](00_fundamentals.qmd#log-likelihood) for background).

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import scipy.optimize as spopt
import sys, os
sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "notebooks"))
from style import apply_style, plot_choices, CHOICE_COLORS, STRATEGY_COLORS
apply_style()
```

## Load synthetic data

```{python}
df = pd.read_csv("data/synthetic_mp.csv")
print(f"{len(df)} trials, strategies: {df['strategy'].unique().tolist()}")
```

## Q-learning model

This mirrors `lee_qlearning_model_nll_w_mask` from `matchingp.modeling.train`.

```{python}
def qlearning_nll(choices, rewards, alpha=0.5, delta_1=1.0, delta_2=-1.0,
                  sess_ids=None):
    """Negative log-likelihood of the Lee Q-learning model.

    Parameters
    ----------
    choices : array of int, 0=L 1=R
    rewards : array of int, 0/1
    alpha   : decay rate
    delta_1 : value update after reward
    delta_2 : value update after no reward
    sess_ids : optional array; resets values at session boundaries

    Returns
    -------
    nll : float
    logit_pR : array, log-odds P(R) per trial
    """
    T = len(choices)
    v_l, v_r = 0.0, 0.0
    nll = 0.0
    logit_pR = np.zeros(T)

    for t in range(T):
        if sess_ids is not None and t > 0 and sess_ids[t] != sess_ids[t - 1]:
            v_l, v_r = 0.0, 0.0

        logit_pR[t] = v_r - v_l
        pR = 1.0 / (1.0 + np.exp(-logit_pR[t]))
        nll -= np.log(pR) if choices[t] == 1 else np.log(1.0 - pR)

        if choices[t] == 0:
            delta_l = delta_1 if rewards[t] == 1 else delta_2
            delta_r = 0
        else:
            delta_l = 0
            delta_r = delta_1 if rewards[t] == 1 else delta_2
        v_l = alpha * v_l + delta_l
        v_r = alpha * v_r + delta_r

    return nll, logit_pR


def fit_qlearning(choices, rewards, sess_ids=None):
    def obj(theta):
        a, d1, d2 = theta
        nll, _ = qlearning_nll(choices, rewards, alpha=a, delta_1=d1,
                                delta_2=d2, sess_ids=sess_ids)
        return nll

    x0 = np.array([0.5, 1.0, -1.0])
    bounds = [(1e-6, 1 - 1e-6), (None, None), (None, None)]
    res = spopt.minimize(obj, x0, method="L-BFGS-B", bounds=bounds)
    return res
```

## Fit to all data

```{python}
choices = (df["choice"].values == "R").astype(int)
rewards = (df["reward"].values > 0).astype(int)
sess_ids = df["session_code"].values

res = fit_qlearning(choices, rewards, sess_ids=sess_ids)
alpha, d1, d2 = res.x
total_trials = len(choices)
ll_bits = -res.fun / (total_trials * np.log(2))
print(f"α={alpha:.3f}  δ₁={d1:+.3f}  δ₂={d2:+.3f}  LL={ll_bits:.4f} bits/trial")
```

## Trial-by-trial predicted P(Right) with choice raster

```{python}
#| label: fig-qlearning-pR
#| fig-cap: "Predicted P(Right) from the fitted Q-learning model overlaid on choice rasters for four example sessions."
_, logit_pR_all = qlearning_nll(choices, rewards, alpha=alpha, delta_1=d1,
                                 delta_2=d2, sess_ids=sess_ids)
pR_all = 1.0 / (1.0 + np.exp(-logit_pR_all))
df["pR_qlearning"] = pR_all

fig, axes = plt.subplots(8, 1, figsize=(12, 10),
                         gridspec_kw={"height_ratios": [2, 1] * 4})
for i in range(4):
    ax_choice = axes[2 * i]
    ax_pr = axes[2 * i + 1]
    sess_code = df["session_code"].unique()[i]
    sess_df = df.loc[df["session_code"] == sess_code].reset_index(drop=True)

    plot_choices(sess_df, shade_strategy=True, fig=fig, ax=ax_choice)
    ax_choice.set_title(f"Session: {sess_code}", fontsize=9)
    ax_choice.set_xlabel("")

    ax_pr.plot(sess_df["pR_qlearning"].values, color="#F45B69", lw=1,
               label="P(R)")
    ax_pr.axhline(0.5, color="grey", lw=0.5, ls="--")
    ax_pr.set_ylim(-0.05, 1.05)
    ax_pr.spines["left"].set_bounds(0, 1)
    ax_pr.set_yticks([0, 0.5, 1])
    ax_pr.set_ylabel("P(R)")
    if i == 0:
        ax_pr.legend(fontsize=7, loc="upper right")

axes[-1].set_xlabel("Trial")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Cross-validated log-likelihood

We compare the Q-learning model's predictive performance using 5-fold
cross-validation (splitting by session).

```{python}
#| label: fig-cv-ll
#| fig-cap: "Cross-validated log-likelihood (bits/trial) by fold."
from sklearn.model_selection import KFold

n_folds = 5
sessions = df["session_code"].unique()
kf = KFold(n_splits=n_folds, shuffle=True, random_state=0)
fold_lls = []

for train_idx, test_idx in kf.split(sessions):
    train_sess = sessions[train_idx]
    test_sess = sessions[test_idx]

    train_df = df.loc[df["session_code"].isin(train_sess)]
    test_df = df.loc[df["session_code"].isin(test_sess)]

    c_train = (train_df["choice"].values == "R").astype(int)
    r_train = (train_df["reward"].values > 0).astype(int)
    s_train = train_df["session_code"].values

    res = fit_qlearning(c_train, r_train, sess_ids=s_train)
    a, d1_, d2_ = res.x

    c_test = (test_df["choice"].values == "R").astype(int)
    r_test = (test_df["reward"].values > 0).astype(int)
    s_test = test_df["session_code"].values

    nll_test, _ = qlearning_nll(c_test, r_test, alpha=a, delta_1=d1_,
                                 delta_2=d2_, sess_ids=s_test)
    ll_bits = -nll_test / (len(c_test) * np.log(2))
    fold_lls.append(ll_bits)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(4, 3))
ax.bar(range(n_folds), fold_lls, color="#F45B69", edgecolor="white",
       linewidth=0.5, alpha=0.85)
ax.axhline(np.mean(fold_lls), color="#333333", ls="--", lw=0.8)
ax.set_xlabel("Fold")
ax.set_ylabel("CV LL (bits / trial)")
ax.set_title(f"Mean = {np.mean(fold_lls):.4f}")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```
